title: Everything bends toward control: who owns the model, and who can switch it off
summary: A spread day that keeps circling ownership and control: ELDR shows that serving a mixture-of-experts model well means routing by which experts are already warm, not just balancing request counts; Portugal ships Amalia, a ~9B national LLM; Together AI raises $800M at $8.3B (Aramco-led) and Microsoft stands up a $2.5B deployment company, both betting the bottleneck is now the last mile, not the model; Zuckerberg tells Meta staff agents haven't accelerated as leadership hoped; and a 'Right to Intelligence' campaign argues for a legal right to run AI locally.

sources.title: ELDR: Expert-Locality-Aware Decode Routing for PD-Disaggregated MoE Serving
Portugal releases Amalia, a 9B national LLM (r/LocalLLaMA)
OpenAI proposed donating 5% of its equity to a US sovereign wealth fund (TechCrunch)
Mark Zuckerberg tells staff AI agents haven't progressed as quickly as anticipated (TechCrunch)
Together AI raises $800M, leaps to $8.3B valuation (TechCrunch)
Microsoft launches its own AI deployment company with $2.5B commitment (TechCrunch)
Right to Intelligence — advocacy for a legal right to run local AI
AI can't be listed as inventor on patents, Japan's top court rules (The Japan News)
Menlo Ventures closes $3B fund; Anthropic stake now ~$14B (news aggregators)
Please stop the AI confidence theater (Elena Verna)

topics: ELDR (Expert-Locality-Aware Decode Routing): for prefill-decode disaggregated MoE serving, equally-loaded decode workers differ in latency by how many DISTINCT experts their batches activate (a single layer ~4.7x slower going from 16 to 128 active experts at fixed batch size); ELDR builds an 'expert signature' from prefill activations (prefill-decode correlation ~0.70-0.92) to route requests to workers already warm on those experts; reduces median time-per-output-token ~7.0-13.9% and per-request active-expert count ~22% vs the strongest load-balancing baseline, with bit-for-bit identical outputs

Portugal releases Amalia, a ~9B national/sovereign LLM (pre-trained on ~4B words, fine-tuned on Portuguese data) built by a consortium of Portuguese universities and research centers; framed as language/civic-text sovereignty and ownership rather than leaderboard competition

Together AI raises $800M Series C at $8.3B valuation, led by Aramco Ventures, to scale open-source model hosting and inference as a neocloud (alongside CoreWeave, Lambda); read as capital betting on inference/serving as the recurring cost center

Microsoft launches Microsoft Frontier Company, a standalone $2.5B business dedicated to helping enterprises deploy AI; part of a pattern with Amazon's ~$1B Forward Deployed Engineering org and OpenAI/Anthropic deployment ventures — the bottleneck moving from model capability to the last-mile deployment gap

Mark Zuckerberg told Meta staff at a Thursday town hall that AI agent development had not 'accelerated in the way' leadership anticipated — a candid recalibration from a major infrastructure bettor, echoing the week's agent-reliability findings

Right to Intelligence: advocacy campaign (petitions, contacting state legislators) arguing for a legal right to run AI models locally on personal hardware; high Hacker News engagement (~514 points), read against export bans/reversals, gated frontier releases, and rising local-memory prices

threads.name: AI economics debate
threads.status: Capital and product both bet on the last mile and the serving layer: Together AI raises $800M at $8.3B (Aramco-led) for open-model inference hosting, Microsoft stands up a $2.5B deployment company (Microsoft Frontier Company) joining Amazon's Forward Deployed Engineering org and the OpenAI/Anthropic deployment ventures, and ELDR shows why shaving inference cost is worth a research team's time.
threads.arc: From 'the model is the product' toward the recurring inference bill and the in-enterprise deployment gap being where the money and the margins actually are — the labs building the consulting arm and the serving stack to capture it.

threads.name: AI sovereignty and open weights
threads.status: Portugal ships Amalia, a ~9B national LLM built by a university consortium, framed as owning your language and civic text rather than topping a leaderboard; Mistral releases Leanstral-1.5 (119B/6B-active MoE) for local runs; and a 'Right to Intelligence' campaign argues for a legal right to run models locally.
threads.arc: The open-weights/sovereignty thread widens from labs and countries releasing runnable weights to an explicit ownership-and-control argument at two scales — national models you can inspect and an individual right to run a model no one can revoke or region-lock.

threads.name: AI agent supply-chain trust
threads.status: Zuckerberg told Meta staff that agent development hasn't accelerated as leadership anticipated — the same wall the week's benchmarks and productivity studies named, now stated from the C-suite: agents work in bounded, supervised lanes but stall on long, open-ended, unsupervised tasks.
threads.arc: The adoption-vs-capability gap gets its most candid admission yet from a top infrastructure bettor: a recalibration (not a eulogy) that the agent roadmaps outran the delivery.

lore.note: Alan's running local-hardware sore spot returned and got named as canon: he keeps an old mining rig he refuses to replace, and the 2026 memory-price spike still visibly bothers him ('it is never letting that go') — the builder's-eye stake in whether running your own model at home stays affordable, tied here to the Right-to-Intelligence campaign.
